A brief EF1 tornado with max wind speeds of 90 MPH touched down on the east bank of the MS river east of Darrow, LA. It damaged four well built, newer, homes. Approximately 20% of each roof was damaged, removed or peeled back. Front porch pillars were moved or ripped away, fences damaged and one garage door blown in. Splatter on the east-northeast sides of the homes was noted as well as multiple pieces of wood embedded into the ground. Its possible this tornado started on the west bank of the river but not enough damage was found to extend it.
Damage around the 1100 block of North Airline Ave in Gramercy with continued damage to the east as well.
Roof and tree damage on N Nobile St in Paulina.

A brief EF0 tornado with max winds of 75 MPH touched down in Gramercy, LA. A few homes, along Millet Dr had minor roof damage and most homes in the neighborhood having fence damage. Debris was noted to be lofted into the back field as well as some splatter seen on the east-northeast side of homes.
Tree down causing additional damage to power pole near intersection of Foy and Dupard.
Weatherflow Mandeville observation recorded a 58 mph wind gust.
Wind gusts near Mandeville estimated between 60 and 70 mph.

A strong EF-1 110 MPH tornado with a path length of of 5.5 miles and a width of 75 yards, occurred over predominantly inaccessible marshes of Plaquemines Parish. High resolution satellite imagery aided in the identification of damage to marsh vegetation after the tornado caused significant damage to a newly rebuilt camp near Lake Laurier. No ground survey could be performed to review the entirety of the structures construction. In consultation with a structural engineer regarding the pictures we had, the rating will be 110 MPH unless additional information can be received. Additional damage to the marsh was identified near Hermitage Road with a few trees apparently damaged as well as the tornado weakened and dissipated before reaching the Mississippi River. Satellite analysis may add additional damage points to the southwest of the damaged track so this track may be extended in future updates.

Start point of an EF-0 tornado that tracked southeast of Denson, LA. NWS storm survey via high resolution satellite imagery identified minor damage to vegetation near Blind River collocated with a tornado debris signature which began at 5:03Z and continued north-northwest before dissipating at around 5:08Z.

Start point of the EF-1 Tornado that tracked from Hammond to near Tickfaw. NWS storm survey determined an EF-1 tornado touched down on the northwest side of the Hammond Northshore Regional Airport just before 4am CDT where patches of large branches were snapped. The tornado continued to the north-northwest across highways 443 and 1064 where it maintained EF-0 strength by snapping small branches. The tornado remained over rural parts of northern Hammond while steadily picking up strength. The tornado was nearing peak intensity as it approached the intersection of Faller Rd and Tycer Ln at 4:06am where a house lost an awning and parts of its roof. The tornado finally reach peak intensity at as it continued to the north-northwest and approaches the intersection of Faller Rd and McManus Dr. Here, a hardwood tree was snapped in the middle. The tornado maintained this peak intensity as it approached Highway 442 where a few more hardwood trees were snapped. The tornado began weakening after this point as is continued to make its way to the north-northwest and across the Tangipahoa River. As it crossed the river, it practically paralleled S Bennett Rd in Independence, LA where it mainly snapped large branches until it lifted shortly before Highway 40 at 4:14am.
Report from Tangi911 of sporadic tree and powerline damage from Hammond to Natalbany. Possible tornado.

Start point of EF-0 tornado that tracked near Loranger. NWS storm survey determined a brief EF-0 touched down just north of Highway 445 on Bailey Loop Rd in Loranger, LA at 4:57am CDT where it broke off patches of large branches. This tornado continued due northward into forested areas of Loranger before making it to Bradford Ln at 5:04am. Here, more patches of small and large branches were broken before the tornado lifted along Highway 40 still at 5:04am.

Start point of the EF-1 tornado that tracked south of Robert. NWS storm survey determined an EF-1 touched down south of Robert, LA at 5:11am CDT along Traino Rd where a few small limbs were snapped. The tornado quickly gained strength as it moved north towards Highway 22. The tornado reached peak intensity as a 90mph EF-1 at 5:14am along Highway 20 just east of the Fairhope Subdivision where a group of softwood trees were snapped. This tornado maintained peak intensity as it continued northward towards Crown Dr at 5:18am. Here more softwood trees were snapped and a roof was partially removed from a manufactured home. The tornado began to weaken and occlude as it moved to the northeast. The tornado broke small limbs and caused small trees to lean in a convergent pattern before lifting at 5:21am just southwest of Jasper Ln south of Robert, LA.

A photographer filmed a waterspout over Lake Pontchartrain just east of the Causeway Bridge a few miles south of Mandeville. Drone footage was key to confirming that this in fact a waterspout and not just a funnel.

A photographer filmed a waterspout over Lake Pontchartrain just east of the Causeway Bridge a few miles south of Mandeville. Drone footage was key to confirming that this in fact a waterspout and not just a funnel.

Tornado damage surveyed on Bear Island Road south of Hwy 22. Preliminary rating is an EF0 with estimated max winds around 75. Mostly tree damage observed however, one house did receive some minor rood damage along with a large metal canopy thrown about 25 to 40 yard to the southeast. Tornado likely began in the marsh before crossing Bear Island Road and continuing to the northeast back into the marsh. More details like path width and length will come out in the next day or two.
The damage path of an EF-1 tornado was identified via high resolution satellite imagery over inaccessible rural marsh areas of Terrebonne Parish, southwest of Humphreys, LA.
Minor roof damage to a mobile home (porch awning removed). Additional damage to sheds and chicken coops. Possible tornado.

Tornado damage surveyed in Tangipahoa parish. Preliminary rating is an EF1 with estimated winds around 95 mph. Damage first noted near on River Rd and Arbuthnott Drive. Strongest damage that we were able to get to was along Dummyline Rd where multiple large trees were snapped. Tornado continued to the north crossing the Tangipahoa River. Most of the path was unsurveyable and satellite imagery will be used in the coming days to see how much tree damage there may have been. Damage was surveyed again in the Camp Living Waters grounds. Mostly trees damage with one tent destroyed. At least 25-30 trees on the camp were down. The tornado lifted shortly as there was no damage surveyed north of Chappepeela Rd.
EM reports observed tornado near Hwy 90 in Gautier by the Junior High School.
The NWS ground survey confirmed an EF-1 tornado with maximum sustained winds of 95mph impacted Gautier, Mississippi in Jackson County. A full report will follow Tuesday, October 28th.
The damage path of an EF-0 tornado was identified via high-resolution satellite analysis east of the Hickory Hills neighborhood in Jackson County, MS.

The NWS ground survey confirmed an EF-0 tornado with maximum sustained winds of 85mph impacted Pascagoula and Moss Point, Mississippi in Jackson County. A full report will follow Tuesday, October 28th.
Emergency Management reports damage to homes on Catalina Street in Pascagoula, MS.
tree branches broken and small tree uprooted.
